Entry.JapaneseText: そうか。 お花畑とは言ってはいけなかったのか
Entry.EnglishText: Should I have not have said they were delusional?

Entry.JapaneseText: うん、今度から気をつけてね
Entry.EnglishText: Yeah, you shouldn't have. Be more careful next time.

Entry.JapaneseText: わかった。 では、今度からは 綺麗なお花畑と言う事にしよう
Entry.EnglishText: Understood. Then from now on, I'll say they're beautifully deluded, then.
Entry.Notes: This is a play on words in JP. お花畑 can mean a flower garden/bed or a field of flowers, but it can also mean delusional or having one's head in the clouds. Milla is saying 綺麗なお花畑, as in, a beautiful flower garden/bed/field, but it doubles over as "well I'll just POLITELY call them deluded, then".

Entry.JapaneseText: そういう問題じゃなくって！
